question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: RetrieveLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of result caching in RetrieveLatest, where every call executes selectLatestWithIndex (ORDER BY index DESC LIMIT 1 on record_file) against the DB, causing sustained DB I/O when called at a moderate but sustained rate without rate limiting?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: FindByIdentifier()] Can an unprivileged external user send FindByIdentifier with a large positive index and a non-empty hash string, causing the system to execute the full selectByHashWithIndex correlated subquery (DB cost) and then perform an application-layer index mismatch check, with no short-circuit before the DB query executes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: findBlockByHash()] Can an unprivileged external user send FindByHash requests with hashes that are valid but correspond to the very first record in record_file, causing the correlated subquery `(select c.consensus_start - 1 from record_file c where c.index = p.index + 1)` to scan for index+1 which exists and requires an additional index lookup, maximizing per-request DB work?
